response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development, including low-latency systems and multi-threaded programming with C/C++ (e.g., National Instruments, Dunnhumby), but most recent roles focus heavily on Node.js, TypeScript, and web/mobile stacks rather than industrial automation or C++-centric systems. While they have some C++ experience, it is not recent or aligned with the core responsibilities of the role. Security and cloud expertise (e.g., IEC 62443, Azure) is lacking. Their strengths lie in web and distributed systems, which do not directly match the job requirements.
strengths: Strong background in low-latency and multi-threaded systems using C/C++ and D (Dunnhumby, NI LabWindows/CVI)
Experience with high-performance databases and asynchronous I/O (Dunnhumby)
Proven track record in scalable architecture and DevOps practices (Altom Consulting, GISCollective)
concerns: Limited recent C++ experience (last significant C++ work was in 2015; recent roles heavily use Node.js/TypeScript)
No evidence of industrial automation, IoT, or message-broker systems experience (key for the role)
Lacks exposure to IEC 62443 or secure development lifecycle practices required for the role
